card.title: mazer: cross-viewport UI reliability and layout hardening
card.summary: Continue the shared viewport contract through the remaining physical-device, installed-PWA, browser-chrome, and native resize/maximize/restore proof boundary.
card.objective: Finish cross-viewport reliability without reopening completed shared-component work or replacing real-surface evidence with deterministic emulation.
acceptanceCriteria: Every primary Mazer surface has an owner, supported viewport category, and no-overlap contract.
Physical iPhone safe areas and installed-PWA shell bands do not obstruct HUD, Pause, maze, controls, overlays, or scroll content.
Native browser resize, maximize, and restore preserve deterministic geometry and current route/overlay state.
Route-aware evidence records viewport, safe-area, mode, overlay, scroll, and layout diagnostics with no actionable console or page errors.
discoveries: PR #60 already completed the shared geometry and deterministic emulated transition foundation.
PRs #70 and #71 completed the Options/Pause flow and fresh-session defaults, so this packet must not duplicate those UI fixes.
Exact live readback found one canonical source thread in in_progress state with nine preserved journals and no identity reason codes.
nextActions: Reconcile the operator physical-device screenshots and current production shell against current main.
Run or extend supported real browser/device proof for safe areas, installed PWA, browser chrome, resize, maximize, and restore.
Apply only shared viewport or shell fixes proven necessary by the exact failing surface.
Close with focused tests, build, route-aware browser proof, production disposition, and exact card lifecycle readback.

entry.headline: Cross-viewport reliability continuation admitted
completed: Reconciled recovered completed cards and removed stale Open projections.
Selected the earliest dependency-safe High-priority critical-path primary.
Correlated the exact live source thread, canonical body, lifecycle state, and journal history.
discovered: The remaining boundary is real-surface proof and any evidence-driven shared shell correction, not another component-layout rewrite.
next: Inspect current production and source against the preserved physical-device evidence, then implement the smallest proof-backed packet.
evidence: DiscordOS recovered completion reconciliation and exact source readback
